taxonomy_v3: Engineering and Development, Cyber Security, Security Engineer
core_skills: Security Engineering, Software Development Life Cycle, Agile Methodologies, Continuous Integration/Continuous Delivery, Java
skill_match_scores: Security Engineering:0.0, Software Development Life Cycle:1.0, Agile Methodologies:1.0, Continuous Integration/Continuous Delivery:1.0, Java:1.0
must_have_quals: Formal training or certification on security engineering concepts and 3 years of applied experience, Experience developing security engineering solutions, Overall knowledge of the Software Development Life Cycle, Solid understanding of agile methodologies such as CI/CD, application resiliency, and security, Experience with Continuous Integration/Continuous Delivery (Jenkins/Selenium Grid), Proficient in coding in one of more languages such as java (Spring, Spring boot)/Python, must have experience in development and deployment of applications in public cloud such as AWS, Database experience (RDBMS or NoSQL) required, Solid understanding of Test Driven Development, JUnit, Mockito, Maven, Repository Management (Git) and designing and developing applications with RESTful Web Services
preferred_quals: Knowledge of Terraform, Docker, Kubernetes is a plus, Monitoring (Dynatrace, Apica, Splunk), Certification in kubernetes or any associate level in AWS is a plus
core_responsibilities: Execute security solutions design, development, and technical troubleshooting with the ability to apply knowledge of existing security solutions to satisfy security requirements for internal clients (e.g., product, platform, application owners), Design, code and test high-quality production code, Perform software maintenance and improvements using knowledge application, data and infrastructure architecture disciplines, Design and develop scalable applications for cloud, Proactively identify hidden problems and patterns in data and use these insights to drive improvements to coding hygiene and system architecture, Resolve high impact production issues and ensure smooth flow of business applications, Drive and select cost-effective security solutions, Implement best practices, new policies, and emerging trends to enhance the strategic roadmap
job_summary: JPMorgan Chase is one of the oldest financial institutions, offering innovative financial solutions. As a Security Engineer III, you will play a critical role in delivering software solutions that prevent misuse and enhance security across various business functions.
